title: Sausage rolls
subtitle: Seasoned sausage meat in a flaky rough puff pastry case
excerpt: Sausage rolls made with a quick rough puff pastry: grated frozen butter folded into flour, rolled and folded several times to build layers. The sausage meat is seasoned with thyme and a grating of nutmeg, wrapped in pastry, and baked until the outside is shatteringly flaky.
sourceNotes: Sausage rolls (seasoned minced pork or sausage meat wrapped in pastry and baked) are documented in British cookery from the early nineteenth century. Mrs Beeton, Book of Household Management (1861), Project Gutenberg #10136, includes sausage roll recipes using puff or rough puff pastry. The modern version with ready-seasoned sausage meat in rough puff pastry is widely reproduced in twentieth-century British baking guides and is fully public-domain.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze unbaked. Bake from frozen at 200°C fan for 35 to 40 minutes.
recipe.batchNotes: Doubles well. Make two rolls; freeze the second batch unbaked.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Assemble the day before and refrigerate, unbaked. Bake on the day.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven, 25 to 30 minutes until deep golden and cooked through

glossaryTerms.definition: Rough puff is a faster alternative to classic puff pastry. Instead of incorporating a block of butter through repeated rolling and folding, rough puff uses grated frozen butter worked quickly into flour with iced water, then rolled and folded three or four times to build the layers. The grated butter melts unevenly during baking, puffing the pastry from inside. Keep everything cold: work quickly, chill the dough between folds if it warms up, and make sure the oven is fully hot before baking so the butter steams rather than leaks.

content.text: The rough puff here is worth making from scratch — it gives a more layered, flakier result than ready-made shortcrust and takes about 20 minutes including resting. If you are short on time, all-butter ready-rolled puff pastry is a reasonable substitute.

items.prepNote: beaten, for egg wash and filling

content.text: Make the rough puff, rest 30 minutes

. Mix the grated frozen {{unsalted-butter}} into the {{plain-flour}} with a pinch of salt until the mixture resembles coarse rubble. Add 6 to 8 tablespoons of iced water, mixing with a palette knife until the dough just comes together. Do not overwork. Roll out to a rectangle, fold in thirds, rotate 90°, and roll again. Repeat twice more. Wrap and chill for 30 minutes.

Mix the {{sausage-meat}} with the thyme leaves from the {{thyme}} sprigs, a grating of {{nutmeg-whole}}, and 1 beaten {{eggs}}. Season with black pepper. Roll the chilled pastry to a large rectangle, roughly 3 mm thick. Cut lengthways into two strips. Divide the filling between the strips, shaping it into a sausage along the centre of each. Fold the pastry over and press the edge to seal. Trim and cut each log into 6 rolls. Brush with beaten egg.

Preheat the oven to 200°C fan. Chill the assembled rolls for 20 minutes. Bake for 25 to 30 minutes until deep golden and the pastry is cooked through. Cool on a rack for at least 10 minutes before eating.
